sections.heading: Summary
lines: CHRM4 | Acute translation guardrails | acute_positive_symptoms

sections.heading: Rationale
lines: xanomeline-based approval supports muscarinic agonism in schizophrenia, while emraclidine keeps selective CHRM4 translation unresolved.
Prioritize acute- and relapse-facing domain profiles while penalizing replay risk, coverage gaps, and unresolved directionality more aggressively.

sections.heading: Risks
lines: Contradiction status: contradicted
Replay status: replay_not_supported
Main contradiction: Repeated adequately engaged selective CHRM4 failures in aligned acute-schizophrenia populations.
Risk: high | Missing required layer groups: biological support (cell_state_support or developmental_regulatory_support). Entity is ineligible for composite ranking until they are sourced.
Risk: medium | Muscarinic relevance is plausible but target-level direction is not yet cleanly pinned down here.

sections.heading: Decision focus
lines: Target: CHRM4

sections.heading: Hypothesis
lines: Test increase_activity CHRM4 via muscarinic_agonism_or_positive_allosteric_modulation (small_molecule, positive_allosteric_modulator) in acute_positive_symptoms.
Direction / modality: increase_activity via muscarinic_agonism_or_positive_allosteric_modulation
Confidence: medium
Evidence basis: xanomeline-based approval supports muscarinic agonism in schizophrenia, while emraclidine keeps selective CHRM4 translation unresolved.

sections.heading: Why it made the packet
lines: Policy signal: Acute translation guardrails scored 0.244 (available) with base 0.364.
Prioritize acute- and relapse-facing domain profiles while penalizing replay risk, coverage gaps, and unresolved directionality more aggressively.
Replay summary: Checked-in history does not support calling this proposal a replay: 1 nonfailure anchor(s) offset the currently available failure analogs (1 remain cautionary).

sections.heading: Evidence anchors
lines: supporting_program: cobenfy-xanomeline-trospium-approval-us-2024 (regulatory_approval, approved_for_adults_with_schizophrenia) - Landmark non-dopaminergic approval relevant to later class-baggage reasoning.

supporting_program: emraclidine-empower-acute-scz-topline-2024 (topline_readout, did_not_meet_primary_endpoint) - Current seed evidence supports recording the miss and keeping muscarinic biology live, but it does not yet justify a molecule-level failure adjudication.

sections.heading: Contradictions and risks
lines: Contradiction status: contradicted
Contradiction condition: Repeated adequately engaged selective CHRM4 failures in aligned acute-schizophrenia populations.
Falsification condition: A well-engaged selective CHRM4 program repeatedly fails while broader muscarinic benefit does not reproduce.
Open risk: high | Missing required layer groups: biological support (cell_state_support or developmental_regulatory_support). Entity is ineligible for composite ranking until they are sourced.
Open risk: medium | Muscarinic relevance is plausible but target-level direction is not yet cleanly pinned down here.

Open risk: medium | Missing source-backed biological support; matched SCHEMA rare-variant support, ChEMBL tractability support, and Open Targets baseline context; missing PGC common-variant support and PsychENCODE biological support.
Open risk: medium | Selective CHRM4 may not capture the broader muscarinic signal seen with xanomeline-based combinations.
Open risk: medium | Tolerability and engagement windows can still distort target-readout interpretation.

sections.heading: Change-my-mind evidence
lines: Escape status: escape_evidence_present

Escape route: cobenfy-xanomeline-trospium-approval-us-2024 - cobenfy-xanomeline-trospium-approval-us-2024 is a checked-in nonfailure anchor in the same biological neighborhood (muscarinic cholinergic modulation). It is not a perfectly clean single-target counterexample.
Needed evidence: A well-engaged selective CHRM4 program repeatedly fails while broader muscarinic benefit does not reproduce.
Needed evidence: Repeated adequately engaged failures in the same target and domain context would strengthen the replay claim.

sections.heading: Decision focus
lines: Target: DRD2
Policy: Acute translation guardrails
Priority domain: acute_positive_symptoms
Decision ask: Decide whether this hypothesis is reviewable enough to advance, hold, or kill.

sections.heading: Hypothesis
lines: Test decrease_activity DRD2 via d2_antagonism_or_partial_agonism (small_molecule) in acute_positive_symptoms.
Direction / modality: decrease_activity via d2_antagonism_or_partial_agonism
Confidence: high
Evidence basis: Decades of antipsychotic use establish symptom control relevance for net DRD2 dampening, with clozapine as a permissive but nonselective anchor.

sections.heading: Why it made the packet
lines: Policy signal: Acute translation guardrails scored 0.302 (available) with base 0.462.
Prioritize acute- and relapse-facing domain profiles while penalizing replay risk, coverage gaps, and unresolved directionality more aggressively.

sections.heading: Evidence anchors
lines: supporting_program: clozapine-clozaril-trs-approval-us-1989 (regulatory_approval, approved_for_treatment_resistant_schizophrenia) - TRS anchor; later curation should treat clozapine exposure as a key boundary for narrower resistant-population labels.

sections.heading: Contradictions and risks
lines: Contradiction status: contradicted
Contradiction condition: A clinically credible schizophrenia program demonstrates benefit from sustained net DRD2 activation in the same use case.
Falsification condition: Robust efficacy without net DRD2 dampening becomes the dominant replicated path for acute antipsychotic benefit.
Open risk: high | Historically important target class but broad mechanistic baggage and limited disease-modifying evidence.
Open risk: high | Class baggage around tolerability and limited disease modification remains substantial.

sections.heading: Change-my-mind evidence
lines: Escape status: escape_unresolved
Needed evidence: Robust efficacy without net DRD2 dampening becomes the dominant replicated path for acute antipsychotic benefit.
Needed evidence: Repeated adequately engaged failures in the same target and domain context would strengthen the replay claim.
